gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/350)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/350)
text: إِنَّ أُمِّي كَانَتْ جَعَلَتْ عَلَيْهَا نَذْراً نَذَرَتْ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي بَعْضِ وُلْدِهَا فِي شَيْ‏ءٍ كَانَتْ تَخَافُهُ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ تَصُومَ ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمَ الَّذِي تَقَدَّمَ فِيهِ عَلَيْهَا مَا بَقِيَتْ فَخَرَجَتْ مَعَنَا إِلَى مَكَّةَ فَأَشْكَلَ عَلَيْنَا صِيَامُهَا فِي السَّفَرِ فَلَمْ تَدْرِ تَصُومُ أَوْ تُفْطِرُ فَسَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ لَا تَصُومُ فِي السَّفَرِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَدْ وَضَعَ عَنْهَا حَقَّهُ فِي السَّفَرِ وَ تَصُومُ هِيَ مَا جَعَلَتْ عَلَى نَفْسِهَا فَقُلْتُ لَهُ فَمَا ذَا إِذَا قَدِمَتْ إِنْ تَرَكَتْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ لَا إِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ تَرَى فِي وَلَدِهَا الَّذِي نَذَرَتْ فِيهِ بَعْضَ مَا تَكْرَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Mahboun, from Ali Bin Raib, from Zurara who said,

‘My mother had made vow upon herself. She vowed for Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic regarding one of her children with regards to something which she had feared over him, that she would be Fasting on that day which had come upon her, for as long as she lived. So she went out with use to Makkah. So that was confusing upon us, for her to be Fasting during the journey. So she did not know when she should be Fasting or breaking it. So I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> about that, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘She should not Fast during the journey. Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic has Placed (dropped) it from her, being her right in the journey, and she should be Fasting what she had made it to be upon herself’. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘So what would be that when the day comes and she leaves that?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No! I<sup>asws</sup> fear that she would see in her child that which she had vowed regarding it, some of what she had left’.
en.sarwar: 24. Ali has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab from Zurarah who has narrated the following: “My mother had made a vow and commitment. She had made a commitment for Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, about one of her children because of fear of something for him. She said that from that day on, she will fast all the time as long as she lives. She came out with us to Makkah, her fasting on the journey made us face questions, and she did not know if she must fast or not. I asked abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about it and he (the Imam) said, ‘She must not fast on a journey. Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, has exempted her from His right during a journey; but she must fast otherwise for what she has committed herself to.’ I then asked, ‘What happens if she ignores her commitment? He (the Imam) said, ‘No, I fear that she may experience in her child that which made her to form such commitment.’”
